core_mechanism: decomposition of a network into topological modules with a core-periphery hierarchy, where a node/module's structural position (tightly-linked core vs. sparsely-linked periphery) predicts and correlates with its dynamical rate and coherence of change
brief_reason: The abstract decomposes the network into modules organized in a core-periphery way and finds that peripheral modules change (evolve) faster and more cohesively than core modules, tying topological position to rate of change.
